title: Chapter Twenty-Nine: The Sword Dragon's Shadow
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"Have you decided on a name for the sword technique?"

"Radiant Sword Style."

Won-chang's face fell slightly. "The resonance is better than I thought. How unfortunate."

"What name were you thinking?"

"'Seizing the World's Radiance and Directly Cutting the Knot.' That was the feeling I received. If it becomes great, it would be a sword that could encompass heaven and earth!"

Yeon-shin drank his tea in silence. The golden shimmer of Jingwei Fu tea -- an offering reserved for the most honoured guests -- said everything about how the Zhongnan Sect now regarded them.

*Bewildering.*

The Sword Immortal's reaction to the Radiant Sword Style had been beyond anything he expected. The brusque old man had embraced him with reddened eyes. They were treating him as an honoured guest for saving his niece and creating a sword technique inspired by their founder's legacy. He had done nothing deliberately for the sect.

"I want to take this golden tea when we return," Won-chang said, entirely at ease.

They set their mission according to the discretion Ma Jin had given them. Prevent civilian damage or deal a meaningful blow to the Tyrant Sword Sect. They had won the Zhongnan Sect's goodwill -- battle news would reach them quickly.

A knock at the door. No -- not a knock. Just a voice, pleasant and clear.

"Sir Jeong?"

Wei Ji Myo-hwa.

He left Won-chang's envious face behind and stepped into the corridor. She stood with the feather decoration in her black hair, one hand resting on her sword handle, the sky-blue uniform bright against the mountain's grey stone.

"I've come to hear what I couldn't discuss before," she said. "About the Sword Master's sword traces."

He nodded.

"Although it's shameful for our sect," she continued, her voice steady, "I want to remove the Sword Master's shadow. In both chivalry and sword path, I must walk my own way. I will become a great master."

Her declaration of ambition before a younger boy was impressive. The caliber matched her reputation.

"I'll support you," Yeon-shin said.

She seemed slightly deflated by his calm tone. Then she composed herself.

"If you help restore the Nine Yang Unified Technique, I promise to serve you as a benefactor until my life ends. You are significantly reducing the time of a life that may not continue long."

*She doesn't know how true that is.*

The most dignified oath in the martial world. Sharing martial arts meant precisely this.

He nodded. "It won't take long."

"How surprising and delightful."

They walked to the cliff's edge together. From a distance, the Sword Immortal watched with his hands behind his back, his face relaxed in a way it had not been since Yeon-shin arrived.

"I'll explain the oral chant first," Yeon-shin said.

Wei Ji Myo-hwa's head whipped toward him. Her hair struck his face.

"What did you just say? Oral chant?"

He touched his cheek where her hair had hit him and glanced at her embarrassed expression.

"It won't be exactly the same. That part seems to be the Sword Dragon's responsibility."

He had traced the sword path's manifestation from the cliff. Reverse-calculated the true energy operation method from the subtleties he understood. He did not discuss how -- he was a key martial artist of Desolate Sect now. He knew the difference between what could and could not be shared.

"In the realm of enlightenment," Wei Ji Myo-hwa breathed, "what a talent..."

"So my part is--"

"I'll tell you the incomplete oral chant, and you refine it closer to completion."

It would be simple for her. Far easier than tracing ancient sword marks and rebuilding the technique from scratch. The sun had begun to set by the time they finished speaking.

---

Wei Ji Myo-hwa disappeared.

Two days passed. According to the Sword Immortal, she had fallen into inner demons.

"She wasn't a child who boasted about martial arts," Jong Yeo-il said. They sat at the cliff's edge in the late afternoon light, watching clouds move over Zhongnan's peaks. "But it seems she was shocked by your talent."

"Best in the world," Yeon-shin repeated. A phrase Jong Yeo-il had used to describe how Wei Ji Myo-hwa had thought of herself.

"Yes. For the first time, she felt her talent was pathetic. After seeing the real thing."

"It's meaningless. The Sword Dragon is several levels above me."

For Yeon-shin, who was pressed for time, this was hard to understand. Invisible talent or age could not substitute for present power.

The Sword Immortal chuckled. "How much of a fatal mistake has heaven made. To give talent that was sufficient, and then a heart that desires insufficiency. It's an unfair thing."

They had grown close. Someone in the sect had said it was like watching a grandfather and grandchild. The warmth was not unwelcome. Jong Yeo-il was easier to be near than Ma Yeon-jeok.

A voice, clear as struck glass.

"Elder Jong. Sir Jeong."

She was already before them. Jet-black hair floating above the sky-blue uniform. Her white face had become emaciated, pale. But her eyes were resolute.

"Since you obtained the oral chant first," she said, clasping fists with Yeon-shin, "you might be able to demonstrate the sword. Can we learn together? The achievement will be faster than training alone."

"I apologize. I don't have enough time to refine my own sword technique."

"Ah..."

Regret in her voice. He added, slowly:

"Since the origin is the same, seeing each other's swords will be sufficient. You can come beside me when I'm training Radiant Sword Style. Practice the Nine Yang Unified Technique."

The way he had learned Cheong Myeong's body technique. Observation. Parallel work. The custom of forbidding martial observation did not carry much meaning to someone who understood his opponents' arts in the first exchange of real combat.

For the first time, true admiration filled Wei Ji Myo-hwa's eyes.

"Sir Jeong is a great master."

"Generosity should be broad to the appropriate extent," Jong Yeo-il said. "The vessel for the world is already complete."

*Misunderstandings are accumulating.*

Yeon-shin put the thought aside and walked to the training ground behind Sunshimgak. He felt Wei Ji Myo-hwa's elegant presence following behind him. When he glanced sideways, she smiled softly and stopped in place.

They drew their swords almost at the same moment.

The Desolate Sword under the sun was more beautiful than he remembered. They said swords grew with their masters. This one had grown through every real battle since he joined Desolate Sect. There might be no blade in the world more suited to him.

*Radiant Light.*

He raised the oral chant. The true energy operation of Radiant Sword Style was nothing like Eternal Blossom Fist -- not the single devastating blow, but a continuous, unbroken flow. Sunlight pooling and rolling in waves along the blade.

The first movements were rough. He felt Wei Ji Myo-hwa's sword beginning beside him -- confident, precise, the overwhelming experience of a seasoned warrior giving her the early lead.

Then the door opened.

Not the grief-door. The other one. The sword-door. The understanding that had been germinating since the cliff face blossomed in his hands, and the Desolate Sword's speed changed, and the swift sword technique melted into his sword method, and the light gathered along the blade became something that was not merely captured but *wielded* --

He danced the sword of the sun.

On the other side of the training ground, Wei Ji Myo-hwa's sword slowly lowered.

She had started ahead of him. And in moments, he had surpassed her -- not just first-level mastery, but second, then third, the Radiant Sword Style obeying him with a fluency that the Nine Yang Unified Technique could not match. Because he was its creator. Because the martial art fitted his body the way a hand fits a glove.

Her eyes blurred.

"What on earth am I..."

The Sword Dragon's focus disappeared. Her sword hung at her side, forgotten.

Yeon-shin did not notice. He was already somewhere else -- inside the sword, inside the light, inside the only thing that mattered.

*I am the master.*

---
